title: InstCombine: Improve SimplifyDemandedFPClass min/max handling
summary: The developers generalized the optimization by replacing specific sign-based checks with a generic ordering comparison using `cannotOrderStrictlyLess/Greater`. Originally, the code handled only cases where one operand was known positive and the other negative. The new approach leverages `KnownFPClass` to determine if one operand is guaranteed to be less than or greater than the other, considering signed zero semantics via `OrderedZeroSign`. This allows the optimization to apply to a wider range of cases, including subnormal values (`psub`/`nsub`) and normals (`pnorm`/`nnorm`), not just simple sign comparisons. The transformation now works for both `maximum/minimum` (which propagate NaNs) and `maximumnum/minimumnum` (which return the non-NaN operand), by checking ordering constraints and ensuring the other operand cannot be NaN when necessary. This generalization makes the optimization more robust and reusable across various floating-point class patterns.
